req.title: Hr-Admin views expense-claim
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a expense-claim through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers viewing an expense-claim via the UI form for an hr-admin. Test design applies EP (valid hr-admin views a claim successfully), NEG (unauthenticated and forbidden-role access), BVA (claim ID at boundary values such as minimum valid ID and non-existent ID), STC (session state transition from unauthenticated to authenticated then viewing), and DT (combination of role x authentication x module-enabled flags). Each test is self-contained using fixtures for session setup and teardown, and assertions cover HTTP status, entity presence, and audit record creation.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_views_expense_claim_success
preconditions: At least one expense-claim record exists in the system
actions: Login as hr-admin via <login_fixture>
Navigate to /expense-claims and select an existing claim
Submit view request for the claim
Capture HTTP response status and returned entity
Query audit log for corresponding audit record
expected: Response body contains the expense-claim entity with a valid id
Audit record is written referencing the viewed claim id and hr-admin actor
trace: R-WEB-044: positive path - authenticated hr-admin views expense-claim
R-WEB-044: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_views_expense_claim_success(hr_admin_page, existing_claim_id, audit_log_client):
    page = hr_admin_page  # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for hr-admin
    claim_id = existing_claim_id  # <fixture>: returns a valid claim id pre-seeded in DB

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/expense-claims" in r.url and r.status in (200, 201)) as resp_info:
        page.goto(f"/expense-claims/{claim_id}")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain entity id"
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(claim_id), "Returned entity id must match requested claim id"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='expense-claim-form']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=claim_id, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written after successful view"
    assert audit_records[-1]["actor_role"] == "hr-admin"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_expense_claim
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
actions: Ensure no authentication cookies or tokens are present
Directly request /expense-claims/{id} without logging in
Capture HTTP response status
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
No audit record is written for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-044: audit record must NOT be written on auth failure
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_expense_claim(base_url, existing_claim_id, audit_log_client):
    # base_url <fixture>: returns the application root URL string
    # existing_claim_id <fixture>: valid seeded claim id
    claim_id = existing_claim_id

    session = requests.Session()  # no auth headers/cookies
    response = session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}")

    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"

    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Response must contain error detail"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=claim_id, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    pre_count = getattr(test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_expense_claim, "_pre_count", 0)
    assert len(audit_records) == pre_count, "No new audit record must be written on 401"

tests.name: test_view_expense_claim_with_boundary_min_valid_id
preconditions: A claim with the minimum valid numeric id (1) exists in the system
actions: Login as hr-admin via <login_fixture>
Request /expense-claims/1 (minimum valid claim id)
Capture HTTP response status and body
Request /expense-claims/0 (below minimum, invalid id)
Capture HTTP response status for invalid id
expected: GET /expense-claims/1 returns HTTP 200 with entity id equal to 1
GET /expense-claims/0 returns HTTP 400 or 404 indicating invalid id
Audit record written only for the successful view of id=1
trace: R-WEB-044: BVA on claim id - minimum boundary (id=1) is valid
R-WEB-044: BVA on claim id - below minimum boundary (id=0) triggers validation error
R-WEB-044: error pathway - 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_view_expense_claim_with_boundary_min_valid_id(hr_admin_session, base_url, audit_log_client):
    # hr_admin_session <fixture>: requests.Session with hr-admin auth headers applied
    session = hr_admin_session

    # At-boundary: id = 1 (minimum valid)
    resp_valid = session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/1")
    assert resp_valid.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 for id=1, got {resp_valid.status_code}"
    body = resp_valid.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == "1", "Entity id must equal requested boundary id"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=1, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must exist for successful view of id=1"

    # Below-boundary: id = 0 (invalid)
    resp_invalid = session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/0")
    assert resp_invalid.status_code in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400 or 404 for id=0, got {resp_invalid.status_code}"
    )

    audit_records_zero = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=0, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    assert len(audit_records_zero) == 0, "No audit record must be written for invalid id=0"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_expense_claim
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: Login as a user with role 'employee' via <login_fixture>
Request /expense-claims/{id} with the employee session
Capture HTTP response status and error body
expected: No audit record is written for the denied access attempt
trace: R-WEB-044: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-044: DT row - authenticated=true, role=employee (not hr-admin), module=enabled => 403
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_expense_claim(employee_session, base_url, existing_claim_id, audit_log_client):
    # employee_session <fixture>: requests.Session authenticated as role=employee
    claim_id = existing_claim_id

    pre_audit = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=claim_id, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    pre_count = len(pre_audit)

    response = employee_session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}")

    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"

    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error", "") or body.get("message", "")).lower()
    assert "forbidden" in error_text or "permission" in error_text or "role" in error_text, (
        "Error body must reference forbidden/role restriction"
    )

    post_audit = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=claim_id, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    assert len(post_audit) == pre_count, "No new audit record must be written on 403"

tests.name: test_view_expense_claim_state_transition_login_then_view
actions: Begin in unauthenticated state - confirm 401 on /expense-claims
Perform login transition to authenticated hr-admin state
Confirm access is now granted - request /expense-claims/{id}
Verify state: claim visible, audit record written
Perform logout transition back to unauthenticated state
Confirm 401 is returned again after logout
expected: Post-login: 200 returned with claim entity and audit record written
trace: R-WEB-044: STC - unauthenticated -> authenticated(hr-admin) -> claim viewed -> unauthenticated
R-WEB-044: audit record written on successful view transition
R-WEB-044: 401 enforced in both pre-login and post-logout states
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_view_expense_claim_state_transition_login_then_view(base_url, hr_admin_credentials, existing_claim_id, audit_log_client):
    # hr_admin_credentials <fixture>: dict with 'username' and 'password' keys
    claim_id = existing_claim_id
    session = requests.Session()

    # State 1: Unauthenticated
    r_pre = session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}")
    assert r_pre.status_code == 401, f"Pre-login must be 401, got {r_pre.status_code}"

    # Transition: Login
    login_resp = session.post(f"{base_url}/auth/login", json=hr_admin_credentials)  # <helper_fn>
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Login must succeed"

    # State 2: Authenticated - view claim
    r_view = session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}")
    assert r_view.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 post-login, got {r_view.status_code}"
    assert "id" in r_view.json(), "Response must contain entity id"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=claim_id, action="VIEW")  # <helper_fn>
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written after successful view"

    # Transition: Logout
    logout_resp = session.post(f"{base_url}/auth/logout")  # <helper_fn>
    assert logout_resp.status_code in (200, 204), "Logout must succeed"

    # State 3: Unauthenticated again
    r_post = session.get(f"{base_url}/expense-claims/{claim_id}")
    assert r_post.status_code == 401, f"Post-logout must be 401, got {r_post.status_code}"
